response.justification: The candidate brings ~15 years of broad full-stack and backend engineering experience, including strong distributed/low-latency work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests per second) and some C/C++ at National Instruments, which partially aligns with the service-reliability and distributed-systems aspects of the role. However, the core of this Principal Growth/Experimentation position—designing experimentation frameworks, feature flighting, telemetry/metrics platforms, statistical analysis, and bias/rollout-risk management—is essentially absent from the resume. There is also limited evidence of IC6/Principal-level cross-team architectural leadership. The relevant scale experience is enough to warrant a conversation, but the specialized experimentation gap is significant.
strengths: Distributed, low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Broad polyglot engineering across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded; meets 8+ years bar with C/C++/Java/JS/TS
Strong software-craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and mentoring/teaching
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or measurement-platform experience
Lacks evidence of metrics/telemetry infrastructure and statistical/experimental analysis aptitude
Limited indication of Principal/IC6-scope cross-team architectural leadership
